briefing.headline: Rig up a Football Jig — steady fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Football Jig is a strong choice for fall with stained water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: Separate the two claws on a Punch Out Craw trailer for a football jig, then hop, drag, and deadstick it along the bottom; in stained water the size and radical claw movement is an ideal combination.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Hackney: 'Use a 3/4 to 1-ounce football head jig on 12-17 pound fluorocarbon to cover water and locate sweet spots along gravel bars or ridges with isolated stumps. Same slow presentation as a Carolin
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: The Punch Out Craw has five manifestations - full glide bait on 4/0 hook and 1/2-oz sinker, separated claws for football jig or Biffle Hardhead, trimmed small craw for swim jig/flipping jig/chatterbait/shakyhead, shortened torso for even ...

KVD: 'Fall spinnerbaits: downsize to 3/16oz in clear water with small blades to match young-of-year baitfish. For heavy grass, go bigger with willowleaf blades. To fish fast and deep, put smaller blades on a 3/4oz head — the Scorcher design lets y...

Palaniuk: 'For southern regions the key player is the shad — I lean towards colors with more flash, silver or white hues with a touch of color. For northern regions, fish follow perch on outside weedlines and transitions to deep water, so I go mor...
A stick bait on a skirtless football head is a productive look for walking down bluff ends

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable fall conditions create the most predictable fall fishing. Bass follow shad migrations into creek arms and pockets, feeding at 3-15ft. Key on points at creek mouths and secondary flats where bait concentrates.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
